1|FROM oven/bun:1.2-alpine AS server-deps
     2|WORKDIR /app
     3|
     4|# NOTE: bun.lock is intentionally NOT required here. The GitHub sync pipeline
     5|# (expo/sync-github.mjs) ignores bun.lock, so the lockfile in this repo can lag
     6|# package.json (e.g. when a new dep like `unpdf` is added). `--frozen-lockfile`
     7|# would then abort the build (the exact cause of prod being stuck on an old
     8|# commit). Resolve fresh from package.json instead so new deps always install.
     9|COPY package.json ./
    10|RUN bun install --production
    11|
    12|# Node 22 ships a native global WebSocket (Node 20 does not). @supabase/supabase-js
    13|# initializes a Realtime WebSocket client in its constructor, so on Node 20 every
    14|# createClient() call threw "Node.js 20 detected without native WebSocket support"
    15|# before any owner-gated request could run. Node 22 (LTS) provides WebSocket natively.
    16|FROM node:22-alpine AS runner
    17|WORKDIR /app
    18|
    19|# ffmpeg + ffprobe power the IVX video pipeline (HLS transcode ladder, thumbnails,
    20|# posters) in backend/services/ivx-video-pipeline.ts and unlock the video worker
    21|# (backend/services/ivx-video-worker.ts). Alpine's ffmpeg package ships both binaries.
    22|RUN apk add --no-cache ffmpeg
    23|
    24|ENV NODE_ENV=production
    25|ENV PORT=3000
    26|ENV HOST=0.0.0.0
    27|
    28|# NOTE: we deliberately do NOT copy the `bun` binary into this Node runner.
    29|# A hard `COPY --from=server-deps /usr/local/bin/bun ...` is FATAL if the source
    30|# path differs between bun image versions, which froze production on the last-good
    31|# image (the landing-SEO auto-deployer never ran). The autonomous lifecycle's
    32|# step 6 already degrades gracefully to the in-process tsx import-smoke check when
    33|# bun is absent (ivx-runtime-resolver), so the Node-only runner is fully
    34|# self-sufficient and the build can never fail on bun setup.
    35|COPY --from=server-deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
    36|COPY package.json ./package.json
    37|COPY server.ts ./server.ts
    38|COPY tsconfig.json ./tsconfig.json
    39|COPY backend ./backend
    40|COPY expo/constants ./expo/constants
    41|COPY expo/shared ./expo/shared
    42|COPY expo/deploy/scripts ./expo/deploy/scripts
    43|# Ship the GitHub-sync scripts so the owner-only sync route
    44|# (POST /api/ivx/autonomy/github/sync, backend/api/ivx-autonomy.ts) can spawn
    45|# `node expo/sync-github.mjs` at runtime. Without these two files the server
    46|# threw MODULE_NOT_FOUND ('/app/expo/sync-github.mjs') and every sync 502'd.
    47|COPY expo/sync-github.mjs ./expo/sync-github.mjs
    48|COPY expo/sync-paths.mjs ./expo/sync-paths.mjs
    49|# Ship the static landing/SEO files so the startup landing-SEO auto-deployer
    50|# (backend/services/ivx-landing-seo-autodeploy.ts) can push robots.txt /
    51|# sitemap.xml / capture.html to S3 with the correct content-types on boot,
    52|# using the AWS credentials that exist on the Render service.
    53|COPY expo/ivxholding-landing ./expo/ivxholding-landing
    54|EXPOSE 3000
    55|
    56|HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=20s --retries=3 \
    57|  CMD wget -qO- "http://127.0.0.1:3000/health" || exit 1
    58|
    59|CMD ["node", "/app/node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs", "/app/server.ts"]

(End of file - total 59 lines)

Wait — Playwright's bundled Chromium is built against glibc and won't run on Alpine musl. So on Alpine you MUST use the system chromium from apk. Playwright supports this via `executablePath` option in `chromium.launch({ executablePath: '/usr/bin/chromium' })`.

    11|
    12|# Node 22 ships a native global WebSocket (Node 20 does not). @supabase/supabase-js
    13|# initializes a Realtime WebSocket client in its constructor, so on Node 20 every

    15|# before any owner-gated request could run. Node 22 (LTS) provides WebSocket natively.
    16|FROM node:22-alpine AS runner

Done. Here's what I built end-to-end:

**Browser-automation service** (`backend/services/ivx-browser-automation.ts`)
- Lazy single shared Chromium browser via `playwright-core` (no bundled browser — uses system Chromium pointed at by `PLAYWRIGHT_CHROMIUM_PATH`)
- `captureScreenshot()` — single URL → base64 PNG + saved file
- `runQAFlow()` — full owner-chat / landing / members / android / iOS flows with per-step screenshots + DOM text transcripts + transcript
- Defensive `getBrowserAvailability()` — reports `playwrightNotInstalled` / `chromiumNotFound` / `launchFailed` instead of crashing

**Owner-gated API** (`backend/api/ivx-browser-automation.ts`)
- `GET  /api/ivx/qa/status` — browser readiness
- `POST /api/ivx/qa/screenshot` `{ url, viewport? }` → PNG
- `POST /api/ivx/qa/run` `{ flow, url?, email?, password?, message? }` → transcript + shots
- `POST /api/ivx/qa/close` — shutdown browser
- All behind `assertIVXOwnerOnly` (same gate as the rest of the owner API)

**Routes** registered in `backend/hono.ts` (lines 516–522, 4220–4227).

**Dockerfile** — `apk add chromium nss freetype harfbuzz ttf-freefont fontconfig noto-fonts-emoji` + `ENV PLAYWRIGHT_CHROMIUM_PATH=/usr/bin/chromium`. Render runs non-root, so the launch args include `--no-sandbox`/`--disable-setuid-sandbox`.

**Dep** — `playwright-core@^1.61.1` added to root `package.json` (the one the Dockerfile installs from).

**Test** — `backend/services/ivx-browser-automation.test.ts`, 7/7 passing (mocked playwright-core + fs).

**Verification**: 1030/1035 backend tests pass. The 5 role-agent failures are a pre-existing parallel-test filesystem race in `logs/audit/role-agents/` — they pass 9/9 in isolation, unrelated to this change.

**To deploy live**: push to GitHub → Render auto-deploys → then call:
```
POST https://api.ivxholding.com/api/ivx/qa/run
Authorization: Bearer <owner-token>
{ "flow": "ownerChat", "email": "...", "password": "...", "message": "QA probe" }
```
Returns real screenshots (base64 + on-disk paths) + a step-by-step transcript proving login → send → AI reply → refresh-persist → members → landing → android → iOS. The owner email/password for login flows read from `IVX_QA_OWNER_EMAIL` / `IVX_QA_OWNER_PASSWORD` env vars on the Render service (add them in the Render dashboard).

One honest caveat: I cannot push to GitHub or trigger Render from this sandbox (no `GITHUB_TOKEN` / `RENDER_API_KEY` in env). Once you push, the `/api/ivx/qa/*` routes go live and you get real visual proof on demand.
